title: Dark-theme animated frontend developer portfolio (React + Tailwind + Framer Motion)

requirements.text: Implement a single-page personal portfolio website in a dark theme with neon accents and a minimal futuristic look: dark background (#0f0f0f / #0b0b0b), cyan/purple/electric-blue accents, glassmorphism cards, soft glow effects, and smooth hover interactions.

quotes:
Create a modern, fully animated personal portfolio website in DARK THEME for a Frontend Web Developer.

Design Style:
- Dark background (#0f0f0f / #0b0b0b)
- Neon accent colors (cyan / purple / electric blue)
- Glassmorphism cards
- Smooth hover effects
- Soft glow effects
- Minimal but futuristic UI

acceptanceCriteria: App uses a consistent dark palette with the specified background values (or extremely close equivalents) across all sections.
Cards/containers use a glassmorphism style (blur + translucent fill + subtle border) that remains readable on dark backgrounds.
Interactive elements (buttons, links, cards) have smooth hover states and subtle glow accents without harming contrast/readability.

requirements.text: Set up Tailwind CSS configuration and global styles to support the dark theme, neon/glow tokens, glassmorphism utilities, custom dark-themed scrollbar, and a subtle noise/grain overlay texture.

quotes:
Output:
- Tailwind config for dark theme

Extra Effects:
- Subtle noise/grain texture
- Custom scrollbar (dark themed)

acceptanceCriteria: Tailwind is configured with reusable theme tokens/utilities for dark surfaces, neon accents, glow shadows, and glassmorphism styling.
A custom scrollbar style is applied globally and matches the dark theme.
A subtle grain/noise effect is visible (and not overpowering) across the site.

requirements.text: Implement a global animation system using Framer Motion, including: smooth page/section transitions, reusable motion variants, scroll-based reveal animations (fade/slide/scale), and a page loader animation shown on initial load.

quotes:
Animations:
- Smooth page transitions
- Scroll-based animations (fade, slide, scale)

Extra Effects:
- Page loader animation

Output:
- Framer Motion animation examples

acceptanceCriteria: Motion variants are centralized and reused across sections (not duplicated ad hoc everywhere).
Scroll-based reveal animations trigger once per section/element and feel smooth on mobile and desktop.
A loader animation is displayed briefly on first load and then cleanly transitions to the hero section.

requirements.text: Create a responsive navbar that supports smooth scrolling to sections and hides/shows based on scroll direction (hide on scroll down, show on scroll up).

quotes:
Performance & UX:
- Fully responsive (mobile, tablet, desktop)
- Smooth scrolling

acceptanceCriteria: Navbar contains links to all required sections and scrolls smoothly to anchors/sections.
On downward scroll the navbar animates out; on upward scroll it animates back in.
Navbar is usable and visually correct on mobile/tablet/desktop (including a mobile-friendly navigation pattern).

requirements.text: Build the Hero section with: large animated name text, role text with typing animation, and two prominent call-to-action buttons labeled “Hire Me” and “View Projects”, including cinematic on-load text animation.

quotes:
1. Hero Section
   - Big animated name text
   - Role text with typing animation
   - Call-to-action buttons (Hire Me / View Projects)

Animations:
- Hero section text animation on load

acceptanceCriteria: Hero name text animates in on load (e.g., staggered reveal/slide/blur) with a premium feel.
Role text uses a visible typing effect (type + delete or type only) and loops or completes gracefully.
CTA buttons are animated (hover/tap) and navigate to Contact/Projects sections respectively (or open the relevant section).

requirements.text: Implement magnetic button interactions for primary CTAs and key interactive elements (subtle attraction to cursor), ensuring accessibility and mobile-safe behavior (disabled or adapted for touch).
acceptanceCriteria: On desktop, magnetic effect is noticeable but subtle and does not cause layout shift.
On touch devices, the effect does not interfere with tapping (either disabled or simplified).
Keyboard focus states remain clear and usable on all buttons/links.

requirements.text: Add a cursor follower animation that complements the neon/glow theme, with appropriate fallbacks on touch devices and without degrading performance.
acceptanceCriteria: A cursor follower element smoothly trails the pointer and visually matches the site theme.
Follower is hidden/disabled on touch devices.
No noticeable lag during normal pointer movement on typical hardware.

requirements.text: Build the About section with an animated profile image, scroll-triggered text reveal, and a set of tech stack icons with hover animations and glow effects.

quotes:
2. About Section
   - Animated profile image
   - Smooth text reveal on scroll
   - Tech stack icons with hover animations

acceptanceCriteria: Profile image has a tasteful animation (float/parallax/scale) and fits the glass/neon aesthetic.
About text reveals on scroll with a smooth, readable motion (not too fast).
Tech icons animate on hover (e.g., lift/rotate/glow) and remain legible on dark background.

requirements.text: Build the Skills section featuring animated progress indicators (progress bars and/or circular loaders) and skill icons with glow hover effects.

quotes:
3. Skills Section
   - Animated progress bars or circular loaders
   - Icons with glow hover effects

acceptanceCriteria: Skills animate into view when scrolled into viewport.
Progress indicators animate from 0 to their target values smoothly.
Skill icons have consistent styling and glow hover effects without visual noise.

requirements.text: Build the Projects section with fully animated project cards, hover tilt interaction, hover overlay showing “Live Demo” and “GitHub” links, and an animated filter control for categories (React / JS / HTML).

quotes:
4. Projects Section
   - Fully animated project cards
   - Hover overlay with live demo & GitHub links
   - Filter animation (React / JS / HTML)

Animations:
- Hover tilt cards

acceptanceCriteria: Projects are displayed as cards with glassmorphism styling and animated entrance/reveal.
Hovering a card shows an overlay with clearly clickable “Live Demo” and “GitHub” links.
Filter chips/buttons animate when changing selection and project grid transitions smoothly between filtered states.
Hover tilt effect is subtle, performant, and disabled/adapted on touch devices.

requirements.text: Build the Experience / Education section as an animated timeline with scroll-triggered reveals and a premium cinematic flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeline items animate into view as the user scrolls (e.g., alternating slide-in, line draw).
Layout remains readable and well-spaced on mobile and desktop.
Timeline animations do not cause content reflow jank.

requirements.text: Build the Contact section with an animated contact form, input focus glow effects, and social media icons with hover animations. Form submission should be handled in-app (e.g., client-side validation + a local success state) without requiring third-party email integrations.

quotes:
6. Contact Section
   - Animated contact form
   - Input focus glow effect
   - Social media icons with hover animation

acceptanceCriteria: Form fields animate on mount/reveal and show a clear glow focus state on focus.
Basic client-side validation exists (required fields, email format).
Submitting the form shows a success message/state and clears or preserves fields in a predictable way.
Social icons animate on hover and are keyboard accessible.

requirements.text: Build the Footer as a minimal dark footer with subtle animated text, matching the futuristic premium tone.

quotes:
7. Footer
   - Minimal dark footer with animated text

acceptanceCriteria: Footer is visually minimal and consistent with the site theme.
Footer text or a small accent element has a subtle animation (not distracting).

requirements.text: Add an animated gradient background (subtle, slow-moving) that complements the dark base and neon accents, and ensure it does not reduce content readability.
acceptanceCriteria: Animated gradient is visible yet subtle and does not overpower text/cards.
Gradient animation runs smoothly and does not cause significant performance issues.

requirements.text: Add parallax scrolling effects for select hero/background elements to enhance cinematic storytelling, with conservative motion and accessibility-friendly behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Parallax is applied to a small number of elements and enhances depth without distraction.
Motion remains smooth on mobile and desktop; effect is reduced/disabled if needed for performance or accessibility preferences.

requirements.text: Ensure overall UX and quality: fully responsive layout (mobile/tablet/desktop), smooth scrolling, SEO-friendly basics (document title, meta description, semantic landmarks/heading structure), and a clean reusable component structure with clear comments.

quotes:
Performance & UX:
- Fully responsive (mobile, tablet, desktop)
- Smooth scrolling
- SEO friendly
- Clean and reusable component structure

Output:
- Full React component structure
- Clean, commented code

acceptanceCriteria: All sections render correctly at common breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop) without overflow issues.
In-page navigation uses smooth scrolling.
Page includes basic SEO metadata and semantic structure (e.g., one H1, meaningful section headings).
Components are organized logically (sections/components/hooks), with readable, commented code where animation logic is non-trivial.

requirements.text: Add required static image assets (generated) to the project under frontend/public/assets/generated and use them in the UI (hero/about/profile, project thumbnails, favicon-style mark, subtle noise texture). Do not serve these images from the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: All images listed in imageRequirements.required exist in frontend/public/assets/generated and are referenced by the UI.
Images load efficiently and do not cause layout shifts (dimensions reserved or constrained).
No backend endpoints are used for image delivery.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui (compose and reuse them as-is).

Do not modify the immutable hook/bootstrap paths listed by the system: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not add third-party authentication (only Internet Identity is supported) and do not add any external email/SMS services for the contact form.
Keep the backend as a single Motoko actor; avoid adding backend features unless strictly necessary for the portfolio UI.
nonGoals: Building a CMS/admin panel for editing portfolio content.
Integrating third-party email delivery (SendGrid/Mailgun/SMTP) or real-time features (WebSockets/chat).
Adding external databases or non-supported backend technologies.
Implementing full multi-page routing unless needed for the requested page transitions (the site may remain a single scrolling page).

required: Futuristic neon monogram logo mark, minimal geometric lines (logo-mark.dim_512x512.png)
Dark cinematic hero background with subtle abstract neon gradients (hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
Stylized profile portrait illustration on dark background with neon rim light (profile-portrait.dim_800x800.png)
Three project thumbnail illustrations with futuristic UI elements, dark theme, neon highlights (project-thumb-1.dim_1200x800.png)
Three project thumbnail illustrations with futuristic UI elements, dark theme, neon highlights (project-thumb-2.dim_1200x800.png)
Three project thumbnail illustrations with futuristic UI elements, dark theme, neon highlights (project-thumb-3.dim_1200x800.png)
